skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X operations through Composio's Twitter toolkit via Rube MCP including posts, search, users, bookmarks, lists, and media management.
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X tasks via Rube MCP with Composio integration for posts, search, users, bookmarks, lists, and media.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 83 external_commands patterns and 1 network pattern, but all are false positives. The detected patterns are markdown code formatting (inline code spans with backticks) documenting MCP tool names, not actual shell command execution. The skill uses Rube MCP to access Twitter API through Composio - legitimate functionality. No malicious intent detected. Minor risk from external API dependency on Twitter and Rube MCP services.

low_findings.title: External API Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill depends on external services: Rube MCP server (https://rube.app/mcp) and Twitter API. Users must trust these third-party services with their data and authentication credentials.

content.user_title: Automate Twitter and X with MCP Tools
content.value_statement: Managing Twitter presence manually consumes significant time and effort. This skill automates posts, searches, bookmarks, lists, and user interactions through Composio integration with Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Create, delete, and look up Twitter posts with optional media attachments
Search recent and full-archive tweets using Twitter search operators
Look up user profiles by username or ID with batch support
Manage bookmarks including listing, adding, and removing saved tweets
Manage Twitter lists including owned, member, pinned, and followed lists
Interact with posts through likes, unlikes, and viewing liked posts
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Twitter OAuth authentication
Twitter API rate limits apply based on access tier (Free, Basic, Pro, Enterprise)
Post creation is not idempotent and may create duplicates on retry
Full archive search requires Academic Research or Enterprise access level

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager
use_cases.description: Schedule and publish posts, monitor brand mentions through search, and organize content with lists for efficient social media management.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals managing brand Twitter presence

use_cases.title: Content Researcher
use_cases.description: Search tweets by topic or hashtag, bookmark relevant posts for later reference, and track specific users or conversations.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and journalists tracking trending topics

use_cases.title: Personal Automation
use_cases.description: Automate personal posting workflows, manage saved tweets in bookmarks, and interact with content from curated lists.
use_cases.target_user: Individual users seeking to streamline Twitter activity

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Post a Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me post a tweet with the text "Hello Twitter! This is my first automated post." using the Twitter automation skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user wants to create their first tweet using MCP tools

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Search Recent Tweets
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent tweets about "AI automation" from the past 24 hours and show me the top 5 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to find recent content on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Post with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload an image from /path/to/image.jpg and create a post with the caption "Check out this amazing view!" attaching the uploaded media.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to post a tweet with an image attachment

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Monitor Brand Mentions
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for tweets mentioning @mybrand from the last 7 days, excluding retweets. For each result, look up the user profile and bookmark important mentions for follow-up.
prompt_templates.scenario: Social media manager monitoring brand reputation and engagement

output_examples.input: Search for tweets about "climate change" from verified users
output_examples.output: Returns 20 recent tweets with author information, engagement metrics, and tweet text matching the search query

output_examples.input: Create a post with media attachment
output_examples.output: Media uploaded successfully with media_id, post created with attached image and confirmation with post URL

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing any workflow
Call TWITTER_USER_LOOKUP_ME to get authenticated user ID before operations requiring user context
Implement deduplication logic for automated posting to avoid duplicate tweets on retry
anti_patterns: Do not retry post creation on timeout without checking if the post was already created
Do not pass integer values for media IDs or user IDs - always use numeric strings
Do not skip OAuth connection verification before running Twitter workflows

faq.question: How do I connect my Twitter account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'twitter'. If not connected, follow the returned OAuth link to authorize. Confirm status shows ACTIVE before running workflows.

faq.question: What are the Twitter API rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits vary by access tier. Free tier has very limited posting (approximately 1,500 posts per month). Check the x-rate-limit-remaining header in API responses for current limits.

faq.question: Can I search tweets older than 7 days?
faq.answer: Recent search covers only the last 7 days. Full archive search requires Academic Research or Enterprise access level for historical tweet data.

faq.question: How do I post a tweet with an image?
faq.answer: First upload the image using TWITTER_UPLOAD_MEDIA, get the media_id from the response, then pass it as a string in the media__media_ids array when creating the post.

faq.question: Why did my duplicate tweet get created?
faq.answer: Post creation is not idempotent. If you retry on timeout, a new post will be created. Implement your own deduplication logic to prevent this.

faq.question: What MCP server do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. No API keys needed - just add the endpoint and authenticate Twitter through the connection flow.
